summaries.content: This lesson introduces facial features and helps students observe the differences in how people look. Everyone has eyes, a nose, ears, a mouth, and eyebrows, but each person's features are shaped differently. Students learn to observe carefully and notice details like eye shape, nose size, and ear shape. The lesson emphasises that these differences are natural and make each person unique.
keyPoints: Every person has the same basic facial features
Each person's features are shaped differently
Careful observation helps us notice details
Our differences make us unique and special

summaries.content: This lesson explores how we get our features from our parents. Children often look like their mother, father, or grandparents because traits like eye colour, hair type, and nose shape are inherited. The lesson also discusses how brothers and sisters may look similar but are never exactly the same, even identical twins have tiny differences.
keyPoints: Children inherit features from their parents
Siblings can look similar but never identical
Even identical twins have tiny differences
Family resemblance connects us to our relatives

summaries.content: This lesson encourages students to appreciate the diversity of human appearances. People from different parts of India and the world may have different skin tones, hair types, and facial features. The lesson teaches that all these differences are beautiful and that we should never judge or make fun of how someone looks. It promotes respect and acceptance of everyone's appearance.
keyPoints: People across India and the world look different
Skin tones, hair types, and features vary by region
All differences in appearance are natural and beautiful
We should respect and accept how everyone looks
